caption: Big Bang
tooltip: The Big Bang is a physical theory that describes how the universe expanded from an initial state of high density and temperature.

caption: Moon walking
tooltip: Commander Neil Armstrong is the first person to step onto the Moon's surface

caption: Coelacanth
tooltip: Fishes that were thought to have become extinct in the Late Cretaceous, around 66 million years ago.

caption: Days of 19 hours
tooltip: The Earth's rotation was faster, resulting in a day length of approximately 18-19 hours.

caption: Sexual Reproduction
tooltip: Evidence suggests that the first instance of sexual reproduction occurred around this time

caption: Supercontinent Rodinia
tooltip: Earth's continental crust collided to form the supercontinent Rodinia, surrounded by a global ocean called Mirovia.

caption: Microbial Land Colonization
tooltip: development of early, non-marine, land-based microbial fossils, indicating that life was beginning to move beyond the oceans.

caption: Giant asteroid mass extinction
tooltip: Chicxulub impactor, the asteroid that caused dinosaurs extinction.

caption: Australopithecus
tooltip: The first Australopithecus specimen, the type specimen, was discovered in 1924 in a lime quarry by workers at Taung, South Africa.

caption: Afropithecus
tooltip: Afropithecus was excavated from a small site near Lake Turkana called Kalodirr in northern Kenya in 1986 and named by Meave and Richard Leakey.

caption: Gorilla
tooltip: The closest relatives of gorillas are the other two Homininae genera, chimpanzees and humans, all of them having diverged from a common ancestor about 7 million years ago.

caption: Kenyapithecus
tooltip: Kenyapithecus was discovered by Louis Leakey in 1961 at a site called Fort Ternan in Kenya.

caption: Art
tooltip: A shell engraved by Homo erectus was determined to be between 430,000 and 540,000 years old.

caption: Archean Eon
tooltip: The Archean Eon marks the formation of Earth's first stable continental crust and the earliest known rock formations. Plate tectonics may have begun during this period.

caption: First stromatolites
tooltip: Cyanobacterial mats begin forming layered stromatolites in shallow seas — the oldest known macroscopic evidence of life on Earth.

caption: Vaalbara Supercontinent
tooltip: One of Earth's earliest proposed supercontinents, Vaalbara, existed during this period, representing the first large-scale assembly of continental crust.

caption: Banded iron formations
tooltip: Massive iron-rich sedimentary deposits form in oceans worldwide as early cyanobacteria produce oxygen that reacts with dissolved iron, leaving striped rock layers.

caption: Great Oxidation Event
tooltip: Cyanobacteria flood the atmosphere with free oxygen, fundamentally transforming Earth's chemistry and causing a mass extinction of anaerobic life — the first major ecological crisis.

caption: Rodinia Breakup
tooltip: The supercontinent Rodinia begins fragmenting, creating vast shallow coastal seas and triggering climate instability that would lead to global glaciations.

caption: Sturtian Ice Age
tooltip: Earth's first major Snowball Earth event encases the planet in ice from poles to tropics. Ocean life is pushed to hydrothermal refugia; lasts ~60 million years.

caption: end of Marinoan Ice Age
tooltip: The second major Snowball Earth glaciation ends dramatically. Rapid deglaciation releases CO₂, causing extreme greenhouse warming and depositing cap carbonate rocks worldwide.

caption: Ediacaran multicellular life
tooltip: First complex multicellular organisms appear — soft-bodied, radially symmetric creatures like Dickinsonia spread across ocean floors, representing the dawn of animal-grade life.

caption: Cambrian
tooltip: Life undergoes explosive diversification as most major animal body plans emerge. Hard shells, eyes, and predator-prey arms races appear for the first time in the fossil record.

caption: Cambrian Explosion peaks
tooltip: Trilobites, anomalocarids, and dozens of new phyla dominate the seas. The Burgess Shale fauna captures this biodiversity. Almost all modern animal groups have their origin here.

caption: Ordovician
tooltip: Marine biodiversity surges in the Great Ordovician Biodiversification Event. Corals, cephalopods, echinoderms, and brachiopods diversify dramatically across shallow tropical seas.

caption: Marine biodiversification
tooltip: Marine animal genera triple in number over ~25 million years, representing the largest surge in biodiversity in Earth's history. New ecological roles and niches appear rapidly.

caption: Ordovician mass extinction
tooltip: Earth's second-largest extinction wipes out ~85% of marine species. Triggered by Gondwana glaciation, falling sea levels drain shallow seas and collapse marine ecosystems.

caption: Vascular plants on land
tooltip: Cooksonia and early vascular plants colonize terrestrial environments during the Silurian, completely transforming land surfaces previously barren of plant life.

caption: Jawed vertebrates
tooltip: Gnathostomes — fish with true jaws — radiate explosively in the Silurian seas, representing a major vertebrate innovation that would lead ultimately to all land vertebrates.

caption: Devonian mass extinction
tooltip: A prolonged extinction lasting ~20 million years eliminates ~75% of species. Reef ecosystems collapse; marine invertebrates suffer especially. Causes include anoxia and cooling.

caption: Fish walks onto land
tooltip: The iconic transitional fossil Tiktaalik bridges fish and tetrapods, possessing fins that function as proto-limbs. Marks the critical step toward vertebrate life on land.

caption: Coal forests
tooltip: Vast equatorial forests of giant ferns, clubmosses, and horsetails dominate the land. Dead plant matter accumulates in swamps, eventually forming most of Earth's coal deposits.

caption: Assembly of Pangaea
tooltip: The landmasses of Laurussia and Gondwana begin merging to form the supercontinent Pangaea, closing the Rheic Ocean and triggering mountain building across multiple continents.

caption: Permian mass extinction
tooltip: Earth's worst mass extinction kills ~96% of marine and ~70% of terrestrial species. Siberian Traps volcanism, ocean acidification, and anoxia combine in a catastrophic extinction cascade.

caption: Carnian pluvial event
tooltip: a period of major change in global climate that coincided with significant changes in Earth's biota both in the sea and on land

caption: Triassic mass extinction
tooltip: Massive volcanism from the Central Atlantic Magmatic Province triggers extinction of ~80% of species, clearing ecological space for dinosaurs to dominate the Jurassic world.

caption: Pangaea splits
tooltip: Pangaea rifts into Laurasia (north) and Gondwana (south), opening the Tethys Sea and Atlantic Ocean. The separation drives evolution of distinct fauna on each landmass.

caption: Birds from dinosaurs
tooltip: Feathered theropod dinosaurs evolve flight during the mid-Jurassic, with Archaeopteryx (~150 Ma) representing the iconic transitional form between non-avian dinosaurs and modern birds.

caption: Hothouse climate
tooltip: Earth enters one of its warmest phases — no polar ice, sea levels ~200m higher than today, and tropical seas extending to mid-latitudes. CO₂ levels are several times modern values.

caption: Oceanic Anoxic
tooltip: A global oceanic anoxic event at ~94 Ma causes widespread marine extinction as deep ocean oxygen is depleted. Associated with massive volcanism from the Caribbean Large Igneous Province.

caption: Thermal Maximum
tooltip: A sudden carbon injection warms Earth by ~5–8°C over ~20,000 years. Ocean acidification, marine extinctions, and rapid mammalian diversification mark this dramatic climate event.

caption: Mammal diversification
tooltip: After the dinosaur extinction, mammals explode into all ecological niches. Placental mammals, rodents, primates, and early hoofed animals diversify rapidly across a warming planet.

caption: India collides with Asia
tooltip: The Indian subcontinent crashes into Eurasia, initiating the Himalayan orogeny — the largest mountain-building event of the Cenozoic, dramatically altering global climate and monsoon patterns.

caption: Whales return to the sea
tooltip: Land-dwelling artiodactyls evolve into early cetaceans like Pakicetus and Ambulocetus, transitioning back to aquatic life in one of evolution's most dramatic documented transformations.

caption: Antarctic ice sheet
tooltip: As Australia separates from Antarctica and the Drake Passage opens, circumpolar ocean currents isolate Antarctica thermally. A permanent ice sheet begins forming, cooling global climate.

caption: Global cooling
tooltip: A major faunal turnover in Europe replaces warm-adapted mammals with cold-tolerant Asian immigrants. Global cooling drives widespread extinctions of tropical species worldwide.

caption: Grasslands and grazing mammals
tooltip: Grasses spread globally as climates cool and dry. Horse, rhino, and tapir lineages diversify; their teeth evolve high crowns to cope with abrasive grass diets, reshaping terrestrial ecosystems.

caption: Miocene
tooltip: The early Miocene sees an explosive radiation of apes across Africa and Eurasia. Over 100 hominoid species eventually evolve during this epoch, setting the stage for human origins.

caption: Kelp forest ecosystems
tooltip: Giant kelp forests evolve in cool, nutrient-rich Pacific coastal waters during the Miocene, creating some of Earth's most biodiverse marine habitats and driving sea otter evolution.

caption: Clothing
tooltip: Humans are wearing clothing by this date.

caption: Writing
tooltip: Sumerian cuneiform writing system and Egyptian hieroglyphs are first used.

caption: Death of Buddha
tooltip: Siddhartha Gautama, the Buddha ('the awakened one'), was a wandering ascetic and religious teacher who lived in the eastern Indo-Gangetic Plains and founded Buddhism.

caption: Crucifixion of Jesus
tooltip: Crucifixion of Jesus, exact date unknown.

caption: Birth of Jesus
tooltip: Widely accepted date (Ussher) for birth of Jesus.

caption: Death of Muhammad
tooltip: Arab religious, military and political leader, as well as the founder of Islam. According to Islam, he was a prophet who was divinely inspired to preach and confirm the monotheistic teachings of Adam, Noah, Abraham, Moses, Jesus, and other prophets.

caption: Dog Pet
tooltip: Domestication of the dog in Siberia.

caption: Dolní Věstonice
tooltip: A hamlet consisting of huts built of rocks and of mammoth bones is founded in what is now Dolní Věstonice in Moravia in the Czech Republic, the oldest human permanent settlement found.

caption: Code of  Hammurabi
tooltip: The longest, best-organized, and best-preserved legal text from the ancient Near East. Written in Akkadian dialect (Old Babylonian), purportedly by Hammurabi, sixth king of the First Dynasty of Babylon.

caption: Code of Ur-Nammu
tooltip: The Code of Ur-Nammu is the oldest known surviving law code. From Mesopotamia and written on tablets, in Sumerian language, it contains strong statements of royal power like 'I eliminated enmity, violence, and cries for justice'.

caption: Rome Foundation
tooltip: Rome founded by Romulus (according to legend). Beginning of the Roman 'Ab urbe condita' calendar.

caption: Charlemagne Emperor
tooltip: Charlemagne, King of the Franks, Lombards, and Emperor of the Carolingian Empire. He united most of Western and Central Europe. The first emperor after the fall of Roman Empire three centuries earlier.

caption: World War II
tooltip: Global conflict between two coalitions. Nearly all of the world's countries participated, with many nations mobilising all resources in pursuit of total war.

caption: World War II over in Asia
tooltip: End of World War II in Asia and beginning of the Occupation of Japan.

caption: World War II over in Europe
tooltip: End of World War II in Europe.

caption: Auschwitz liberated
tooltip: The Holocaust (in Hebrew Shoah) was the genocide of European Jews during World War II. Between 1941 and 1945, Nazis and collaborators systematically murdered some six million Jews.

caption: Smartphone
tooltip: The iPhone is the first smartphone with a world scale.

caption: Darwin's theory
tooltip: Charles Darwin publishes On the Origin of Species.

caption: CRISPR gene editing
tooltip: Jennifer Doudna / Emmanuelle Charpentier: CRISPR gene editing

caption: AIDS
tooltip: The AIDS epidemic officially begins in the United States, having originated in Africa; making this to be an ongoing pandemic.

caption: Mayflower
tooltip: Forty-one Pilgrims aboard the Mayflower, anchored off Massachusetts, signed a compact calling for a 'body politick.'

caption: Napoleonic Wars
tooltip: War breaks out between Britain and France; this is considered by some to be the beginning of the Napoleonic Wars.

caption: Napoleon's death
tooltip: Napoleon Bonaparte dies in exile on the island of Saint Helena.

caption: ESA
tooltip: The European Space Agency (ESA) is a 23-member international organisation devoted to space exploration. Founded in 1975.

caption: Roman Apogee
tooltip: The Empire sees a period of unprecedented stability and prosperity known as the Pax Romana. Rome reached its greatest territorial extent under Trajan.

caption: Muqaddimah
tooltip: The Muqaddimah, written by Ibn Khaldūn, is a foundational work in sociology, history, and political theory. It analyzes how societies form, grow, and decline.

caption: death of Louis XIV
tooltip: King of France for 72 years. His legacy includes French colonial expansion, the conclusion of the Thirty Years' War, influence on fine arts and architecture (including the Palace of Versailles).

caption: Saudi Arabia
tooltip: The First Saudi State is founded by Mohammed Ibn Saud.

caption: Chess
tooltip: Sasanian Empire King Khosrow I is represented on his throne before a chessboard, 10th century AD.

caption: Seismometer
tooltip: Seismometer in Han dynasty China, built by Zhang Heng. An inverted pendulum acting on inertia, like the ground tremors from earthquakes, to dislodge a metal ball by a lever trip device.

caption: Ukrainian-Soviet war begins.
tooltip: War between the Ukrainian Republic and the Bolsheviks. It ensued soon after the October Revolution when Lenin dispatched Antonov's expeditionary group to Ukraine and Southern Russia.

caption: Common Era
tooltip: The dating system globally adopted for international commerce and communication. Afghanistan, Iran, Ethiopia, and Nepal have not adopted it. Some countries also use their own traditional calendars for religious or cultural purposes.

caption: 5 million Humans
tooltip: It has been estimated that there were some five million people at the time of the Last Glacial Maximum. That is an average growth rate of 0.027% p.a. from the beginning of the Neolithic to the Middle Bronze Age.

caption: Laschamp event
tooltip: The Laschamp event is the first geomagnetic excursion (short reversal of Earth's magnetic field) studied (we only know a few reversals). Effects are not considered strong enough to significantly affect natural or human history.

caption: Pottery
tooltip: Earliest pottery use, in Xianren Cave

caption: Holocene global warming
tooltip: An abrupt period of global warming begins. This is taken as the beginning of the Holocene geological epoch.

caption: 40 million Humans
tooltip: It has been estimated that there were some forty million people by 5000 BC. That is an average growth rate of 0.027% p.a. from the beginning of the Neolithic to the Middle Bronze Age.

caption: 100 million Humans
tooltip: It has been estimated that there were around 100 million people by 1600 BC. That is an average growth rate of 0.027% p.a. from the beginning of the Neolithic to the Middle Bronze Age.

caption: Aeschylus
tooltip: Athenian playwright Aeschylus wins a poetry prize.

caption: Eiffel Tower
tooltip: Eiffel Tower is inaugurated in Paris.

caption: Plato in Syracuse
tooltip: Plato, having left Athens on Socrates' death to visit Megara and possibly Egypt, travels to Syracuse at the invitation of Dionysius I's brother-in-law Dion.

caption: Otto I
tooltip: Otto I (Otton) Crowned Holy Roman Emperor

caption: Quebec foundation
tooltip: The city of Quebec was founded by Samuel de Champlain.

caption: Pocahontas
tooltip: Pocahontas, daughter of the leader of the Powhatan tribe, married English colonist John Rolfe in Virginia.

caption: Manhattan
tooltip: Dutch explorer Peter Minuit landed on what is now Manhattan.

caption: Harvard College
tooltip: Harvard College in Cambridge, Mass., held its first commencement.

caption: Mississipi reached
tooltip: French explorer Robert La Salle reached the Mississippi River.

caption: Pasteur
tooltip: Louis Pasteur creates the first successful vaccine against rabies for a young boy who had been bitten 14 times by a rabid dog.

caption: Dr. Jekyll, Mr. Hyde
tooltip: Forty-one Pilgrims aboard the Mayflower, anchored off Massachusetts, signed a compact calling for a 'body politick.'

caption: Jack the Ripper
tooltip: Jack the Ripper murders occur in Whitechapel, London.

caption: Aspirin
fragment: History:~:text=]: 27-,The word Aspirin was Bayer's brand name
countries: Germany, Iran, Iraq, Kuwait, Egypt, France

caption: Oscar Wilde
tooltip: Trial of Oscar Wilde and premiere of his play The Importance of Being Earnest.

caption: Tour de France
tooltip: The first Tour de France is held.

caption: Panama Canal
tooltip: Construction of the Panama Canal begins.

caption: Ford
tooltip: The Ford Motor Company invents the Model T.

caption: Tunguska
tooltip: The Tunguska impact devastates thousands of square kilometres of Siberia.

caption: America's boy scouts
tooltip: Boy Scouts of America is founded.

caption: Amundsen at South Pole
tooltip: Roald Amundsen first reaches the South Pole.

caption: Kuomintang
tooltip: The Kuomintang, the Chinese nationalist party, is founded.

caption: Passenger pigeon
tooltip: Martha, last known passenger pigeon, dies.

caption: Italy enters war
tooltip: The Treaty of London brings Italy into the war.

caption: First use of tanks
tooltip: First use of tanks at the Battle of Flers-Courcelette.

caption: USA in WWI
tooltip: USA joins the Allies for the last 17 months of World War I.

caption: World War  I end
tooltip: Armistice of 11 November 1918 ends World War I. Casualties: 30 million military, 8 million civilian deaths.

caption: Spanish flu considered over
tooltip: Spanish flu pandemic. Death estimates range from 17-50 million (possibly 100 million), making it the deadliest pandemic in history.

caption: League of Nations founded
tooltip: League of Nations founded in Paris.

caption: Hitler becomes führer
tooltip: Adolf Hitler becomes Führer of the Nazi Party as hyperinflation in the Weimar Republic begins.

caption: Walt Disney Company
tooltip: The Walt Disney Company is founded.

caption: Kemal Atatürk
tooltip: Turkish War of Independence ends; Kemal Atatürk becomes the first President of the newly established Republic of Turkey; Ankara replaces Istanbul as its capital.

caption: Rhapsody In Blue
tooltip: George Gershwin composes Rhapsody In Blue.

caption: Mickey Mouse
tooltip: Mickey Mouse is created at the Walt Disney Studio.

caption: Vatican City
tooltip: Vatican City is recognised as a sovereign state.

caption: Academy Awards
tooltip: The first Academy Awards are presented.

caption: 1st FIFA World Cup
tooltip: First FIFA World Cup hosted in Uruguay.

caption: Mao Zedong
tooltip: The Chinese Soviet Republic is proclaimed by Mao Zedong.

caption: Long March
tooltip: Mao Zedong begins the Long March.

caption: Bonnie and Clyde
tooltip: Bonnie and Clyde are shot to death in a police ambush.

caption: Nazi invasion of Poland
tooltip: Nazi invasion of Poland triggers the beginning of World War II in Europe.

caption: Molotov-Ribbentrop Pact
tooltip: Molotov-Ribbentrop Pact between Germany and the Soviet Union.

caption: Soviets attack Finland
tooltip: Winter War between Soviet Union and Finland ends with a costly victory for the USSR.

caption: Nazi invasion of France
tooltip: Nazis invade France, the Netherlands, Denmark and Norway.

caption: Battle of Britain
tooltip: Battle of Britain, the first entirely aerial military campaign, becomes the first significant defeat for the Axis powers.

caption: Churchill Prime Minister
tooltip: Winston Churchill becomes Prime Minister of the United Kingdom.

caption: Trotsky assassinated.
tooltip: Trotsky and Lenin were considered the two most prominent figures in the Soviet state from 1917. Ideologically a Marxist and a Leninist, Trotsky's ideas inspired a school of Marxism known as Trotskyism.

caption: Pearl Harbor
tooltip: Attack on Pearl Harbor, which leads to the USA joining World War II.

caption: London Blitz ends.
tooltip: The Blitz is a bombing campaign against the UK that lasted eight months (7/9/1940-11/5/1941). The name is a shortened form of Blitzkrieg, a term used in the popular press to describe a German style of surprise attack.

caption: Operation Barbarossa starts
tooltip: Hitler commences the Nazi invasion of the Soviet Union.

caption: Battle of Stalingrad start
tooltip: The Battle of Stalingrad was a major battle on the Eastern Front of World War II, beginning when Nazi Germany and its Axis allies attacked and became locked in a protracted struggle with the Soviet Union for control over Stalingrad (now Volgograd).

caption: Battle of Stalingrad end
tooltip: Battle of Stalingrad ends with over two million casualties and the retreat of the German Army.

caption: Colossus 1st computer
tooltip: First electronic computer prototype, Colossus Mark 1, works in December 1943 and is in use at Bletchley Park by early 1944.

caption: Leningrad liberated
tooltip: The Siege of Leningrad ends with Soviet victory after over a million deaths.

caption: Battle of Leyte starts
tooltip: American and Filipino troops begin the Battle of Leyte in the Philippines.

caption: Death of Adolf Hitler.
tooltip: Adolf Hitler, chancellor and dictator of Germany during the Nazi era from 1933 to 1945, committed suicide by gunshot to the head in the Führerbunker in Berlin after it became clear that Germany would be defeated during the Battle of Berlin.

caption: Potsdam Conference
tooltip: Potsdam Conference divides Europe into Western and Soviet blocs. United Nations founded.

caption: I have a dream
tooltip: Martin Luther King Jr. delivers 'I Have a Dream' speech at the March on Washington for Jobs and Freedom.

caption: Leonid Brezhnev
tooltip: Leonid Brezhnev ousts Khrushchev and assumes power in the Soviet Union.

caption: Lucy
tooltip: Discovery of 'Lucy' (Australopithecus afarensis) in Tanzania's Olduvai Gorge.

caption: Star Wars
tooltip: Star Wars quickly becomes the highest-grossing film of all-time.

caption: Pope John Paul II
tooltip: John Paul I and then John Paul II become Pope.

caption: Thriller (M. Jackson)
tooltip: Michael Jackson releases his landmark album Thriller, the best-selling album of all time.

caption: Berlin Wall
tooltip: Fall of the Berlin Wall; Revolutions of 1989 and collapse of the Soviet Bloc in Europe, which lead to the end of the Cold War.

caption: Osama bin Laden killed
tooltip: US Navy SEALs: Osama bin Laden killed

caption: World Wide Web.
tooltip: Tim Berners-Lee invents the World Wide Web.

caption: Death of Diana
tooltip: Diana, Princess of Wales is killed in a car accident in Paris.

caption: Columbine massacre
tooltip: Columbine High School massacre in Colorado, United States.

caption: Los Angeles riots
tooltip: Los Angeles riots over the acquittal of those involved in the beating of Rodney King.

caption: Waco siege
tooltip: The Waco siege ends after a 51-day standoff, leaving 86 people dead.

caption: 1st black hole discovered
tooltip: Cygnus_X-1, the first black hole is discovered.

caption: Dolly
tooltip: Dolly the sheep becomes the first successful cloned mammal.

caption: Maradona Goal
tooltip: Diego Maradona: Goal of the Century (World Cup)

caption: Maradona's death
tooltip: Diego Maradona: Death / global mourning legacy

caption: Wayne Gretzky scoring record
tooltip: Wayne Gretzky: Sets NHL scoring records

caption: Zidane's Headbutt
tooltip: Zinedine Zidane: Headbutt at World Cup Final

caption: Shohei Ohtani Unanimous MVP
tooltip: Shohei Ohtani: Unanimous MVP / historic free agent deal

caption: Osaka US Open
tooltip: Naomi Osaka: US Open title / mental health advocacy

caption: Djokovic: Wimbledon
tooltip: Novak Djokovic: Wimbledon title / rise to world No. 1

caption: Federer World No. 1
tooltip: Roger Federer: Becomes World No. 1 tennis

caption: Gene-edited human babies
tooltip: He Jiankui: First gene-edited human babies (controversial)

caption: Hitler Chancellor
tooltip: Adolf Hitler: Appointed Chancellor of Germany

caption: Great Wall construction
tooltip: Qin Shi Huang: Great Wall construction ordered

caption: Google founded
tooltip: Larry Page / Sergey Brin: Google founded

caption: James Webb Space Telescope
tooltip: NASA: James Webb Space Telescope launched

caption: Deep Blue defeats Kasparov
tooltip: IBM Deep Blue: Deep Blue defeats Kasparov

caption: Apple Computer founded
tooltip: Steve Jobs / Steve Wozniak: Apple Computer founded

caption: Human genome sequenced
tooltip: Human Genome Project: Human genome fully sequenced

caption: Fall of Kabul
tooltip: Taliban: Fall of Kabul / US withdrawal

caption: Franz Ferdinand assassination
tooltip: Gavrilo Princip: Assassination of Franz Ferdinand

caption: Siege of Sarajevo begins
tooltip: Bosnian Serbs: Siege of Sarajevo begins

caption: Battle of the Nile
tooltip: Horatio Nelson: Battle of the Nile

caption: Plains of Abraham Battle
tooltip: Wolfe / Montcalm: Battle of the Plains of Abraham

caption: Battle of the Somme
tooltip: Haig / Von Below: Battle of the Somme

caption: Battle of El Alamein
tooltip: Montgomery / Rommel: Battle of El Alamein (2nd)

caption: Light Brigade Charge
tooltip: Lord Cardigan: Charge of the Light Brigade

caption: Mandela president
tooltip: Nelson Mandela: South Africa first Black president

caption: Battle of Crecy
tooltip: Edward III of England: Battle of Crecy

caption: Battle of Agincourt
tooltip: Henry V of England: Battle of Agincourt

caption: Battle of Blenheim
tooltip: Duke of Marlborough: Battle of Blenheim

caption: Battle of Hastings
tooltip: William the Conqueror: Battle of Hastings

caption: Babe Ruth 60 home runs
tooltip: Babe Ruth: 60 home runs in a single season

caption: Red Sox World Series
tooltip: Boston Red Sox: World Series championship (curse broken)

caption: Aaron Judge 62 home runs
tooltip: Aaron Judge: 62 home runs (American League record)

caption: Caitlin Clark NCAA
tooltip: Caitlin Clark: NCAA women's basketball scoring record

caption: LeBron James comeback
tooltip: LeBron James: Cleveland Cavaliers NBA Championship (3-1 comeback)

caption: Joe Burrow: Super Bowl
tooltip: Joe Burrow: Super Bowl appearance first full season

caption: Emmitt Smith: NFL
tooltip: Emmitt Smith: NFL all-time rushing yards record

caption: Lamar Jackson: NFL
tooltip: Lamar Jackson: NFL MVP unanimous vote

caption: Bo Jackson
tooltip: Bo Jackson: Heisman winner / NFL-MLB two-sport star

caption: Jackie Robinson MLB
tooltip: Jackie Robinson: First Black MLB player (Brooklyn Dodgers)

caption: Cal Ripken Jr
tooltip: Cal Ripken Jr.: 2,131 consecutive games streak

caption: McGwire 70 home runs
tooltip: Mark McGwire: 70 home runs in a season

caption: Willie Mays World Series
tooltip: Willie Mays: The Catch - World Series Game 1

caption: Michael Jordan Rookie of the Year
tooltip: Michael Jordan: NBA Rookie of the Year

caption: Cleveland Cavaliers comeback
tooltip: Cleveland Cavaliers: NBA Finals comeback from 3-1 deficit

caption: Caitlin Clark season record
tooltip: Caitlin Clark: NCAA scoring season record

caption: Muhammad Ali Rumble
tooltip: Muhammad Ali: Rumble in the Jungle

caption: Lance Armstrong 5 Tour
tooltip: Lance Armstrong: 5th consecutive Tour de France

caption: FIFA Women's World Cup
tooltip: US Women's Soccer: FIFA Women's World Cup win

caption: Nicklaus 18 Major
tooltip: Jack Nicklaus: 18 Major Championship titles record

caption: Jack Nicklaus Masters at 46
tooltip: Jack Nicklaus: Masters title at age 46

caption: Tiger Woods: Masters at 21
tooltip: Tiger Woods: Masters win at age 21 (record low)

caption: Scheffler Masters
tooltip: Scottie Scheffler: Masters victory / World No. 1

caption: D. Johnson, player of the Year
tooltip: Dustin Johnson: PGA Player of the Year

caption: Danica Patrick pole position
tooltip: Danica Patrick: Daytona 500 pole position (first woman)

caption: Serena Williams: Grand Slam
tooltip: Serena Williams: Calendar Year Grand Slam

caption: Billie Jean King
tooltip: Billie Jean King: Battle of the Sexes match

caption: Sampras 6th consecutive #1
tooltip: Pete Sampras: Year-end World No. 1 (6th consecutive)

caption: Battle of Bouvines
tooltip: Philip II of France: Battle of Bouvines

caption: Battle of Dien Bien Phu
tooltip: Vo Nguyen Giap: Battle of Dien Bien Phu

caption: Fall of Berlin Wall
tooltip: East Germany: Fall of the Berlin Wall

caption: Battle of Lepanto
tooltip: Don John of Austria: Battle of Lepanto

caption: Battle of Mosul
tooltip: Iraqi forces / US: Battle of Mosul (ISIS recapture)

caption: Battle of the Boyne
tooltip: William III: Battle of the Boyne

caption: Israeli invasion of Lebanon
tooltip: Sharon / Begin: Israeli invasion of Lebanon

caption: Oslo Accords
tooltip: Yasser Arafat / Rabin: Oslo Accords signed

caption: Hamas attack on Israel
tooltip: Hamas: Hamas attack on Israel / Gaza War begins

caption: Japan invades Manchuria
tooltip: Kwantung Army: Mukden Incident (Japan invades Manchuria)

caption: Battle of Port Arthur
tooltip: Togo Heihachiro: Battle of Port Arthur (Russo-Japanese)

caption: Battle of Gaugamela
tooltip: Alexander the Great: Battle of Gaugamela

caption: Battle of Legnica
tooltip: Batu Khan: Battle of Legnica (Mongol invasion)

caption: Korean War begins
tooltip: Kim Il-sung / Truman: Korean War begins

caption: Bangladesh Liberation War
tooltip: Mukti Bahini: Bangladesh Liberation War begins

caption: Battle of Warsaw
tooltip: Pilsudski: Battle of Warsaw (Miracle on Vistula)

caption: Battle of Gravelotte
tooltip: Von Moltke the Elder: Battle of Gravelotte (Franco-Prussian War)

caption: Wagner Group mutiny
tooltip: Yevgeny Prigozhin: Wagner Group mutiny in Russia

caption: Battle of Poltava
tooltip: Peter the Great: Battle of Poltava

caption: Battle of Culloden
tooltip: Duke of Cumberland: Battle of Culloden

caption: Battle of Kursk
tooltip: Zhukov / Von Manstein: Battle of Kursk

caption: Spanish Civil War begins
tooltip: Francisco Franco: Spanish Civil War begins

caption: Syrian Civil War
tooltip: Assad regime: Syrian Civil War begins

caption: ISIS Caliphate
tooltip: Abu Bakr al-Baghdadi: ISIS Caliphate declared

caption: US Independence Declaration
tooltip: Founding Fathers: US Declaration of Independence

caption: Civil War
tooltip: Civil war in the United States between the Union ("the North") and the Confederacy ("the South"), which was formed in 1861 by states that had seceded from the Union to preserve slavery in the United States, which they saw as threatened.

caption: Statue of Liberty
tooltip: A colossal sculpture of a robed and crowned woman on Liberty Island in New York Harbor. The copper-clad statue, a gift to the United States from the people of France, was designed by French sculptor Frédéric Auguste Bartholdi.

caption: Suez Canal
tooltip: An artificial sea-level waterway in Egypt, connecting the Mediterranean Sea to the Red Sea through the Isthmus of Suez and dividing Africa and Asia.

caption: Battle of Fort Sumter
tooltip: Beauregard: Battle of Fort Sumter (Civil War start)

caption: Afghanistan War
tooltip: George W. Bush: Afghanistan War begins

caption: Battles of Lexington Concord
tooltip: Minutemen: Battles of Lexington and Concord

caption: Battle of Chosin Reservoir
tooltip: MacArthur / Peng Dehuai: Battle of Chosin Reservoir

caption: Bay of Pigs Invasion
tooltip: Kennedy / Castro: Bay of Pigs Invasion

caption: Battle of the Bulge
tooltip: Patton / Von Rundstedt: Battle of the Bulge

caption: Iran Hostage Crisis
tooltip: Khomeini followers: Iran Hostage Crisis begins

caption: Operation Desert Storm
